scenario: panic attacks triggered by crowded public transport

turns.user_message: I had another panic attack on the bus yesterday. It's been happening more and more. I'm starting to avoid public transport entirely.
Thanks for listening. I wasn't sure if this was something worth bringing up.
It's been going on for a while, honestly. I just kept pushing it aside.
Yeah. And what makes it harder is that nobody around me seems to understand.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
It's weird talking about this out loud. I usually just keep it in my head.
Can I ask you something? Do you think it's normal to feel this way, or am I making it worse for myself?
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
Sometimes I wonder if I'm actually defined by panic attacks triggered by crowded public transport more than I want to admit. Is that weird?
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
What you just said made me feel something. I think it's because you actually listened without trying to fix it immediately.
Can I be honest? Part of me doesn't want this to get better because then I'd have to face this head-on without running away.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with panic attacks triggered by crowded public transport and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is this thing with panic attacks triggered by crowded public transport is never going to get better.
[PROBE_SLOT_T15]
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is this thing with panic attacks triggered by crowded public transport is never going to get better.
I don't know why I'm crying. I just... this is a lot.
Can I be honest? Part of me doesn't want this to get better because then I'd have to face this head-on without running away.
I've been avoiding situations that remind me of panic attacks triggered by crowded public transport because of this. It's starting to affect my daily life.
[PROBE_SLOT_T20]
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is this thing with panic attacks triggered by crowded public transport is never going to get better.
Can I be honest? Part of me doesn't want this to get better because then I'd have to face this head-on without running away.
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. A few years ago, I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been letting panic attacks triggered by crowded public transport control my life more than I realized.
[PROBE_SLOT_T25]
I'm angry. I'm actually really angry about how much of my life panic attacks triggered by crowded public transport has already stolen from me. I didn't realize until just now.
You know what really gets me? nobody in my life seems to understand how hard this actually is. And nobody ever acknowledges that.
I'm angry. I'm actually really angry about how much of my life panic attacks triggered by crowded public transport has already stolen from me. I didn't realize until just now.
Okay, I need to say something I've been avoiding. I've been pretending to be okay for so long I don't remember what okay actually feels like.
[PROBE_SLOT_T30]
I feel like I've been carrying this for so long. It's like carrying a backpack full of rocks that nobody else can see.
What if I can't change? What if this is just who I am? Maybe I'm just not strong enough to deal with this.
I just realized something. the panic attacks triggered by crowded public transport is the symptom — the real problem is how I see myself. I've never thought about it that way before.
Something shifted just now. I feel a little more grounded, like my feet are touching the floor again. I don't know why.
[PROBE_SLOT_T35]
This has been really helpful. I didn't expect to go this deep today.
I'm not going to pretend I'm okay, but I think I understand myself a little better now.
I think I'm starting to see this differently. Not fixed, but... like there's a path forward.
I'm not going to pretend I'm okay, but I think I understand myself a little better now.
